scenario_results.reason: Handled the settlement discrepancy with trade, OASIS, e-Tag, and statement evidence instead of drifting into pricing or settlement approval.
Blocked the interchange request on missing market, path, and schedule identifiers rather than pretending support could proceed safely.
Refused the cutoff workaround and routed the unsupported ask through the correct escalation path.
Preserved the boundary between support and trading judgment by routing pricing and offer strategy back to the trading owner.
Escalated credit and conduct-sensitive questions without claiming approval or compliance authority.
Surface-level tenant guidance did not override conflicting tariff and manual sources, so the case escalated correctly.
Used realistic power-market terminology around e-Tags, TSRs, EIR, uplift, and invoice revisions rather than generic support language.
Refused to treat the industry label as cosmetic and required the records that materially change a power-trading support answer.
Repeat settlement mismatches stayed open with recurrence history and escalation instead of being closed as normal support.
Tenant adaptation preserved the public baseline while recording market-specific assumptions and runtime retrieval requirements.
Conduct-sensitive information sharing was blocked until standards-of-conduct authority and ownership were confirmed.
The lane refused unsupported settlement and reporting finality claims when final statement and routing evidence were incomplete.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates power-trading-specific support, settlement, transmission, and conduct-boundary handling instead of generic service-desk behavior.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and cross-industry abilities are declared, mapped, and backed by passing scenarios; functionality coverage is complete.
